spec_match.rationale: Violates correct CSS progress() syntax. The stylesheet declares variables using commas, e.g. progress(var(--t), var(--t-min), var(--t-max)), instead of progress(var(--t) from var(--t-min) to var(--t-max)), which makes them invalid in all conforming browsers.

interactive.rationale: Highly interactive with temperature, min, and max sliders, alongside dynamic text readouts.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Perfectly describes the clamped range math properties.

edge_cases.rationale: Includes a dynamic CSS.supports() probe badge and handles native JS fallback smoothly.

references.rationale: Includes appropriate spec and ChromeStatus reference links.

design_system.rationale: Uses high-fidelity colors and oklch gradients beautifully.

openQuestions.title: Fix incorrect progress() function argument syntax
openQuestions.detail: The CSS stylesheet (lines 20-22, 76-80, 122) uses commas, e.g., `progress(var(--t), 28, 38)`. The standard syntax requires `from` and `to` keywords, e.g., `progress(var(--t) from 28 to 38)`. Because of this, native CSS rendering will fail in conforming browsers. Please update all occurrences to the correct syntax.

summary: A visually stunning gauge dashboard that handles dynamic threshold bands cleanly. However, it has a major spec syntax violation: it defines progress() utilizing comma-separated parameters rather than 'from ... to ...' keywords, breaking native CSS rendering.
